name: Creole Poached Chicken Breasts- WW
recipeIngredient: 1 green bell pepper, seeded and chopped
16 ounces skinless boneless chicken breast halves
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the chicken and pat dry with paper towels.
2. In a large nonstick skillet, heat the oil over medium heat. Sauté the onions, green bell pepper, celery, and carrot until tender, about 4-5 minutes. Stir in the stewed tomatoes, low-sodium chicken broth, chopped parsley, ground thyme, salt, and cayenne pepper.
3. Add the chicken breast halves to the skillet and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat and cover partially. Poach until the chicken is cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
4. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the chicken to a platter. Allow the sauce to simmer uncovered until it reduces by half, about 4-5 minutes. Return the sauce to the skillet and pour over the poached chicken.
Makes 4 servings. Each serving contains 182 calories (22% from fat), 4 grams fat (1 gram saturated fat), 16 grams carbohydrate, 22 grams protein, 541 mg sodium, 49 mg cholesterol, 82 mg calcium, and 5 grams fiber.
Recipe from "Weight Watchers New Complete Cookbook" by Macmillan. Tested by Kathleen O'Gorman for the Free Press Test Kitchen.
